intro: Brachystelma chloranthum is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as a leafy-stemmed, cactus-like succulent native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Brachystelma chloranthum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Brachystelma chloranthum is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
